title: Women of Angkor: celestial or real? - EYHO Tours
highlights:
Apsaras is a catch-all term since not all Angkorian female images are apsaras. In keeping with the Hindu association of apsaras to dance, only those shown dancing or poised to dance are considered apsaras. Female figures depicted individually or in groups who are standing still and facing forward like temple guardians are called devatas. Devata (photo: R.F. Ebert)

Devata (photo: Matthew Laird)

title: Apsaras or Devatas? The Celestial Ladies of Angkor - Lucas Varro
highlights: explained. We call them apsaras.Or devatas.Or sometimes, with reverent hesitation, both.  ...    alike—this one arises more often than I might have guessed:“What’s the difference between an apsara and a devata?” There was a time I answered with clarity.Apsaras, I would say, are celestial dancers—mythic nymphs of Indra’s court, flying through sacred skies or caught mid-dance, radiant and playful.Devatas are temple guardians—standing still, more solemn than seductive, more poised than joyful. They carry flowers or staffs. Their beauty is spiritual, their gaze  ...    In short: devatas do not dance. Apsaras never quite stand still.  ...    Second: Devata is too broad. It refers to all minor deities—male and female, guardians and musicians, wind spirits and divine warriors. Apsaras themselves are one type of devata. title: The Meaning and Importance of Devatas in Khmer History
highlights: alten Tempel des Khmer-Reiches, insbesondere des weltbekannten Angkor Wat und Banteay -Srei -Tempel sind mit atemberaubenden Schnitzereien von himmlischen Wesen geschmückt, die als bekannt als Devatas. Diese spirituellen Figuren haben einen besonderen Platz in Khmer -Kunst, Architektur und Mythologie und symbolisieren göttliche Vormundschaft, Weiblichkeit und kosmische Ordnung. Die Devatas spiegeln nicht nur die religiöse Hingabe des  ...    In der hinduistischen und buddhistischen Kosmologie sind Devatas himmlische Wesen oder kleinere Gottheiten, die den höheren Göttern dienen und das kosmische Gleichgewicht aufrechterhalten. Der Begriff "Devata" stammt aus dem Sanskrit -Wort "Deva", was Gottheit oder göttliches Wesen bedeutet. Während Apsaras (Himmlische Tänzer) werden oft als anmutig und ätherisch dargestellt. title: Apsara Archives - Devata.org
highlights: 1,800 devata  ...    The Khmer civilization that grew to unify most of Southeast Asia between the 8th-14th centuries respected women. Today, we still see their temples filled with images of sacred women –termed devata, apsara or Khmer goddesses – who embody the feminine forces of the universe. While women and goddesses appear as icons in many ancient and modern societies, the Khmers gave sanctified women dominance over their state temples more consistently and more visibly than any other  ...
